isi-pompasi.title: Heat Pump Systems
isi-pompasi.description: Air-source energy conversion for sustainable climate control.

header.title: Heat Pump Systems
header.description: Sustainable climate control through air-source energy conversion...

vision.title: Not Just an Appliance —
vision.description: A heat pump is not a white goods product — it's the heart of your building. At Likya Mechanical, we don't rely on catalogue specs. We focus on real site conditions. From Fethiye's humidity levels to static pressure requirements, every parameter is factored into our design to deliver a true energy hub.

f1.title: Air-Source Heat Pump Installation
f1.desc: Quiet, high-efficiency outdoor unit installation fully calibrated for Fethiye's climate conditions.

f2.title: Heating & Cooling System Integration
f2.desc: Seamless hydraulic integration with underfloor heating, fan-coil units, or existing radiator systems.

f3.title: Energy Efficiency Consulting
f3.desc: Room-by-room heat loss analysis and COP optimisation to minimise electricity consumption.

f4.title: Scheduled Maintenance & Servicing
f4.desc: Hydraulic balancing, filter cleaning, and refrigerant checks to extend system lifespan.

f5.title: Fault Diagnosis & Repair
f5.desc: Fast, accurate diagnosis with advanced equipment and repairs using genuine replacement parts.

individual.title: Residential & Solar Integration
individual.desc: For villas, our priority is acoustic comfort and maximum efficiency. We integrate solar collectors as the primary heat source with the heat pump acting as a smart backup unit.

commercial.title: Commercial & Operational Maintenance
commercial.desc: For businesses, uninterrupted energy supply is non-negotiable. Through cascade architecture and scheduled hydraulic inspections, we guarantee the system maintains day-one performance.

q1.q: Is an air-source heat pump sufficient for Fethiye's winter conditions?
q1.a: Absolutely. Mild climates like Fethiye's are among the most ideal locations in the world for air-source heat pumps. Since outdoor temperatures rarely drop below freezing here, the system enters defrost mode infrequently and consistently achieves its highest COP (efficiency) values.

q2.q: Can I connect a heat pump to my existing radiator system?
q2.a: Heat pumps perform best with low-temperature systems (35–40°C), which is why they pair perfectly with underfloor heating. That said, using our high-temperature output models or by recalculating your existing radiator capacity, efficient operation with radiators is entirely achievable.

q3.q: How much can I save by combining solar energy with a heat pump?
q3.a: Solar collectors prepare domestic hot water 'for free' during sunny hours. The heat pump only kicks in on cloudy days or at night to top up the temperature when solar input falls short. This hybrid setup can reduce your annual hot water costs by up to 80%.

q4.q: Will the outdoor unit disturb my neighbours with noise?
q4.a: The next-generation units we install at Likya Mechanical operate at 35–40 dB(A) — roughly equivalent to a soft whisper. Anti-vibration mounts used during installation completely prevent structural noise transmission.

q5.q: What is the system's lifespan, and does it require maintenance?
q5.a: A properly engineered and hydraulically balanced system has an average service life of 15–20 years. For optimal efficiency, an annual hydraulic filter clean and outdoor unit inspection is all that's needed.

cta.title: Schedule a Technical Consultation
cta.description: Speak with our engineering team to carry out a thermodynamic analysis of your project and identify the most efficient hybrid system for your needs.

yangin-sistemleri.title: Fire Safety Systems
yangin-sistemleri.description: Life- and property-safe fire protection solutions, fully compliant with current regulations.

header.title: Fire Safety Systems
header.description: Turnkey fire protection solutions — from design to commissioning — fully compliant with the Turkish Regulation on Fire Protection of Buildings and international standards.

vision.title: More Than an Installation —
vision.description: Fire systems leave no room for error. At Likya Mechanical, we carry out hydraulic calculations to international standards (NFPA) and in full compliance with local regulations to maximise life safety in your buildings.

f1.title: Fire Detection & Alarm Systems
f1.desc: Smart automation solutions using smoke, heat, and flame detectors to identify fires at the earliest possible stage.

f2.title: Pump Stations & Water Storage
f2.desc: Diesel or electric redundant fire pump sets and modular tank installations ensuring constant system pressure.

f3.title: Sprinkler Systems
f3.desc: Wet, dry, and deluge sprinkler installations providing automatic, zone-based fire suppression.

f4.title: Gaseous Suppression Systems
f4.desc: FM200 and Novec gas-based protection for server rooms, archives, and other areas where water-based suppression could cause damage.

f5.title: Periodic Maintenance & Inspection
f5.desc: Routine testing and reporting to TS EN 12845 standards to keep systems ready to perform at any moment.

f6.title: Project & Engineering Services
f6.desc: Hydraulic calculation reports, isometric drawings, and regulation-approved fire evacuation scenarios.

f7.title: Fire Hose Cabinets & Hydrant Installations
f7.desc: Indoor hose reel cabinets and outdoor hydrant networks meeting fire service standards for water distribution.

individual.title: Residential & Boutique Projects
individual.desc: In villas and residential developments, we unite aesthetics with safety. Concealed sprinkler heads and sleek fire cabinets protect without compromising the architectural finish.

commercial.title: Commercial & Industrial Facilities
commercial.desc: Complex solutions to NFPA standards for hotels and factories. Cascade pump systems and integrated detection units keep your operation protected around the clock.

q1.q: Is fire protection installation legally required in buildings?
q1.a: Yes. Under the Turkish Regulation on Fire Protection of Buildings, fire installations are a legal requirement for all structures above certain occupancy, height, and hazard class thresholds.

q2.q: Why do fire pumps need to be tested regularly?
q2.a: Fire pumps can sit idle for extended periods. Risks such as shaft seizure or control panel failure can go undetected without routine checks. Weekly and monthly tests are essential to guarantee the pump will operate when it matters most.

q3.a: No. Sprinkler heads only activate when a specific temperature threshold (typically 68°C) causes the internal glass bulb to expand and shatter. They will not discharge on their own unless subjected to physical impact.

q4.q: Can a fire suppression system be retrofitted into an existing building?
q4.a: Yes. Our engineering team surveys your building's structure and architecture to plan suitable pipe routes and deliver a fully compliant retrofit project with minimal disruption.

q5.q: Are your maintenance reports accepted by official authorities?
q5.a: Yes. Periodic maintenance forms and test reports issued by Likya Mechanical are officially recognised for fire service inspections and insurance policy requirements.

cta.title: Free Risk Assessment
cta.description: Book a technical site survey to improve your building's fire safety and verify compliance with current regulations.

yerden-isitma.title: Underfloor Heating Systems
yerden-isitma.description: Comfortable, efficient underfloor heating installations tailored to your space.

header.title: Underfloor Heating Systems
header.description: Dust-free, healthy climate control through uniform heat distribution. Fully integrated solutions with heat pumps and solar energy.

vision.title: Every Corner of Your Home,
vision.description: While conventional radiators dry out the air and overheat upper rooms, underfloor heating works through natural convection — keeping your feet warm and your head cool. At Likya Mechanical, we carry out room-by-room heat loss analyses to determine the optimal modulation ranges for your project.
vision.stats: Up to 25% Less Energy Than Radiators

f1.title: Thermostats & Automation
f1.desc: Precision room thermostats with mobile access and manifold automation, allowing individual temperature control in every room.

f2.title: Hydronic & Electric Systems
f2.desc: High-efficiency mechanical installation of wet or dry underfloor heating systems, selected to suit your specific usage requirements.

f3.title: Integration with Existing Systems
f3.desc: Flawless hydraulic and automation compatibility with heat pumps, solar collectors, or existing boiler systems.

f4.title: Floor Preparation & Insulation
f4.desc: High-density insulation boards and perimeter edge strips installed to standard, preventing heat loss into the subfloor.

f5.title: Maintenance & System Flushing
f5.desc: Scheduled maintenance including chemical flushing and corrosion inhibitor treatment to prevent limescale and sludge build-up in pipework.

f6.title: Engineering & Project Services
f6.desc: Room-by-room heat loss calculations and correct pipe spacing determination using specialist engineering software.

f7.title: Oxygen Barrier Pipe Technology
f7.desc: EVOH-layer oxygen barrier pipes laid as single continuous runs to protect the system from corrosion and extend its service life.

individual.title: Hydronic Underfloor Heating
individual.desc: For new-build projects, screed-embedded installation delivers the highest thermal mass. This is the most efficient heating model for use with a heat pump.

commercial.title: Dry System (Low Profile)
commercial.desc: A fast-responding heating solution using slim panels for existing buildings without breaking the floor, or where ceiling height restrictions apply.

q1.q: Does underfloor heating take a long time to warm up?
q1.a: Hydronic underfloor heating works on thermal mass, so it takes longer to reach temperature than radiators — but it also retains heat far longer after switching off. With smart automation, the system's operating schedule is optimised to turn this characteristic into an advantage.

q2.q: Should pipes be laid under furniture?
q2.a: At Likya Mechanical, we do not lay pipes beneath kitchen units or fixed wardrobes. This protects the furniture from heat damage and eliminates unnecessary energy consumption.

q3.q: What floor covering works best with underfloor heating?
q3.a: Ceramic, marble, and natural stone have the highest thermal conductivity and deliver the best results. That said, underfloor-heating-compatible laminate flooring options can also provide a very comfortable finish.

q4.q: Is there a risk of pipes bursting or leaking?
q4.a: The oxygen barrier pipes we use are laid as a single, continuous run with no joints beneath the screed. A pressure test is carried out before screed is poured and the system is left under pressure — giving it a service life equivalent to the building itself.

q5.q: Does underfloor heating stir up dust?
q5.a: Quite the opposite. Because it operates at much lower surface temperatures than radiators, it does not drive air circulation that lifts dust. It is the healthiest heating method available for people with asthma or allergies.

cta.title: Schedule a Thermal Design Consultation
cta.description: Get in touch with us to prepare the most comfortable underfloor heating design for your home and determine the ideal material combination for your project.

havuz-tesisati.title: Pool Mechanical Systems
havuz-tesisati.description: Professional filtration, disinfection, and heating solutions for crystal-clear, healthy water.

header.title: Pool Mechanical Systems
header.description: Flawless circulation and intelligent disinfection systems. Healthy, sustainable pool enjoyment backed by Likya Mechanical.

vision.title: More Than Water —
vision.description: The heart of any pool is its plant room. At Likya Mechanical, we go beyond clear-looking water — we ensure it remains biologically safe. From backwash flow rates matched to your pool volume to ideal circulation times, every detail is engineered to specification.
vision.stats: Up to 30% Energy Savings Through Optimised Circulation

f1.title: Pool Heating Systems
f1.desc: High-efficiency heating solutions integrated with heat pumps or solar collectors, extending your swimming season to all four seasons.

f2.title: Circulation Pump & Sand Filter Installation
f2.desc: Quiet, correctly sized circulation pumps and high-capacity quartz sand filters installed and calibrated to match your pool volume.

f3.title: Pool Mechanical Design
f3.desc: Flow rate calculations, pipe diameter optimisation, and plant room layout planning to full engineering standards.

f4.title: Filtration & Disinfection Systems
f4.desc: Salt chlorine generators, automatic pH dosing systems, and UV sterilisation units for hands-free, maximum water hygiene.

f5.title: Maintenance & Overhaul
f5.desc: Sand replacement, leak checks, pump overhaul, and pre-season technical preparation services.

f6.title: Lighting & Accessories
f6.desc: Installation of energy-efficient RGB LED lighting, stainless steel ladders, and pool inlet fittings.

f7.title: Automation & Remote Control
f7.desc: Integrated control panels letting you manage pool temperature, chlorine levels, and lighting from your smartphone.

individual.title: Overflow Systems
individual.desc: The ideal choice for aesthetics and maximum filtration. Surface debris is continuously collected via perimeter channels and directed to a balance tank for professional-grade water quality.

commercial.title: Skimmer Systems
commercial.desc: A practical, direct-suction circulation model for projects requiring lower costs and faster installation, with no balance tank required.

q1.q: Does a pool heat pump significantly increase electricity bills?
q1.a: Pool heat pumps draw heat energy from the outside air, making them up to 80% more efficient than conventional electric heaters. They can produce approximately 5–6 kW of thermal energy for every 1 kW of electricity consumed.

q2.q: How often does the sand in a filter need replacing?
q2.a: To maintain its filtration capacity, quartz sand should typically be replaced every 3–5 years depending on usage levels. Switching to glass media can extend this interval to up to 10 years.

q3.q: Do I still need to add chlorine if I have a salt chlorine generator?
q3.a: A salt chlorine generator converts salt in the water into natural chlorine, providing continuous disinfection. Under normal conditions, it eliminates the need for manual chlorine dosing by up to 90%.

q4.q: How can I tell if my pool has a water leak?
q4.a: If your pool is losing more than 1 cm of water per day beyond normal evaporation, there may be a leak in the plumbing or the pool shell. We can pinpoint the exact location of any leak in the pipework using a pressure test.

q5.q: Why is winter maintenance important for pool systems?
q5.a: In areas with frost risk, draining pumps, filters, and pipework and protecting valves over winter significantly extends system lifespan and prevents costly repair bills at the start of the next season.

cta.title: Free Pool Site Survey
cta.description: Get in touch with our engineers to modernise your pool's mechanical infrastructure or receive a quote for a new project.

havalandirma.title: Ventilation Systems
havalandirma.description: Healthy indoor air quality through fresh air management and climate control solutions.

header.title: Ventilation Systems
header.description: Fresh air supply, exhaust systems, and heat recovery solutions for industrial, commercial, and residential projects.

vision.title: For Your Buildings,
vision.description: In enclosed spaces, air quality is not just about comfort — it's a health necessity. At Likya Mechanical, we use heat recovery systems to minimise energy losses while delivering precisely calculated fresh air volumes to meet every space's specific needs.

f1.title: Air Handling Units & Heat Recovery
f1.desc: Air handling units that condition outdoor air before supplying it to the space, and energy-efficient heat recovery devices that reclaim thermal energy from exhaust air.

f2.title: Ductwork & Installation
f2.desc: Galvanised, stainless, or fabric duct systems optimised for airflow velocity with a guaranteed airtight seal.

f3.title: Mechanical Ventilation Design
f3.desc: Engineering calculations for required air change rates and duct pressure losses, tailored to space type — kitchen, office, car park, and more.

f4.title: Filtration & Clean Air Systems
f4.desc: High-standard air quality through G4, F7, or HEPA filtration options that capture dust, pollen, and harmful particulates.

f5.title: Exhaust & Extraction Systems
f5.desc: Powerful fan and extractor maintenance for everything from commercial kitchen hoods to industrial facilities, removing stale air and odours effectively.

f6.title: VAV & CAV Automation
f6.desc: Variable or constant air volume control units for intelligent, demand-driven ventilation management that saves energy without compromising air quality.

f7.title: Jet Fans & Car Park Ventilation
f7.desc: High-performance systems for enclosed car parks providing daily CO extraction and smoke evacuation in the event of fire.

individual.title: Commercial & Industrial Ventilation
individual.desc: High-capacity exhaust and fresh air systems for factories, restaurants, and gyms. Professional solutions to tackle odour and humidity problems.

commercial.title: Residential Fresh Air
commercial.desc: Silent systems for modern homes and villas providing fresh air supply without energy waste, using heat recovery ventilation units (HRV).

q1.q: What does a heat recovery ventilation (HRV) unit actually do?
q1.a: An HRV unit transfers the heat from stale exhaust air to the incoming fresh air stream. This means in winter, instead of losing all that warmth when ventilating, you recover 70–80% of the heat energy that would otherwise be wasted.

q2.q: Why do ventilation ducts need to be cleaned?
q2.a: Dust and bacteria that accumulate in ducts over time degrade indoor air quality and can trigger allergic reactions. Clogged filters also put unnecessary strain on fan motors, increasing energy consumption.

q3.q: What should I watch out for in commercial kitchen ventilation?
q3.a: Fans must be rated for hot, grease-laden exhaust air, and easily cleanable grease filters are essential. Equally important is a fresh air supply to balance the negative pressure created by the extraction system.

q4.a: When engineered correctly and fitted with appropriate silencers and insulation, noise levels stay comfortably within comfort limits. Variable frequency drives (VFDs) allow further fine-tuning of sound levels.

q5.q: How often does the system need maintenance?
q5.a: Depending on usage intensity, filters should be checked every 3–6 months. Fan motors and belt-pulley mechanisms are recommended for annual preventive maintenance.

cta.description: Book an on-site survey and technical design consultation for your business or home's ventilation requirements.

about.title: About Us
about.description: Mechanical engineering and installation across Fethiye and the Southern Aegean: heat pumps, underfloor heating, fire suppression systems, ventilation, and pool plumbing — all delivered with an engineering-first approach.
about.mission: Whether it's a villa or a large hotel operation, our benchmark never changes: the lowest possible investment-to-operating-cost ratio for the owner, and uninterrupted comfort for the occupant. Our goal is systems that work correctly, last long, and cost little to run. The heating and cooling, ventilation, fire protection, and pool plumbing systems we design — using engineering calculations specific to Fethiye's and the Southern Aegean's climate and infrastructure — deliver both simultaneously.
about.vision: A system bearing the Likya Mechanical signature should stand as the reference point for the most reliable and highest-return mechanical investment in the region. What sustains that claim is the measurable engineering output and repeatable technical accuracy we deliver on every project. Every system we hand over becomes the reference for the next.
about.whyDescription: At least six concrete reasons to choose us...

why.label: Engineering first, installation second
why.description: Every project undergoes heat loss analysis, hydraulic schematic design, and capacity calculations before a single tool hits the site. Our designs are grounded in field reality, not catalogue recommendations.

why.label: Designed around Fethiye
why.description: The region's humidity levels, seasonal temperature range, and coastal influence are all built into our calculations. We work with local data — not generic coefficients.

why.label: Certified materials, documented workmanship
why.description: We work exclusively with manufacturers holding CE and TSE certification. Every member of our installation team holds a specialist qualification relevant to the system they install.

why.label: One point of contact, start to finish
why.description: The engineer who surveys your project is the same team that designs and delivers it. We remain reachable for servicing and maintenance long after handover.

why.label: Transparent pricing and process
why.description: A detailed survey report is provided before any quote is submitted. Any changes to costs or timelines are communicated to you in advance — never after the fact.

why.label: Efficiency is always part of the equation
why.description: Optimisation consulting is included in every project. Solar integration, COP analysis, and hybrid systems are standard options we explore as a matter of course.
